title: User, Agent, and Tool Authority, Least Agency, Dry Runs, and Revocation · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent organization supplies a different user population, task distribution, tool set, risk tier, model response, resource budget, accessibility need, and failure condition and transfers user, agent, and tool authority, least agency, dry runs, and revocation into a changed least-agency authorization decision engine. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of least-agency authorization decision engine with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress throug…
objectives: Distinguish authenticated user, affected subject, agent, application, tool, service identity, resource owner, and approver.
Issue task-scoped, resource-scoped, time-bounded capabilities and expose only tools permitted for the current state.
Classify pure reads, sensitive reads, reversible writes, irreversible writes, external communication, financial, identity, and security effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Authority Principals
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab agent engineering team handles case 9383 during a controlled support-operations rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate user, agent, and tool authority with least privilege, effect classes, dry runs, denials, approvals, and revocation; model output and tool arguments remain untrusted until validated; then change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-deny-escalate-revoke-1woain
# Competency: agentpy-deny-escalate-revoke.
# Case 1794: a privacy-minimized nonprofit workflow.
# Operating constraint: model output and tool arguments remain untrusted until validated.
# Changed case: change model capability, API version, schema, or provider state and revalidate the adapter.
def evidence_agentpy_deny_escalate_revoke_1woain(requested_actions: int = 24, authorized_actions: int = 10, denied_actions: int = 8, approval_actions: int = 6, privilege_leaks: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1woain = "1woain-niaow1-1794"
    assert evidence_variant_1woain
    evidence_axes_1woain = (
        "authority1woain",
        "interaction1794",
        "toolniaow1",
        "state1woain1794",
        "evaluation17941woain",
        "recoveryniaow11794",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1woain)) == 6
    if requested_actions < 1 or authorized_actions < 0 or denied_actions < 0:
        return False, requested_actions, "authorization-count"
    if authorized_actions + denied_actions + approval_actions != requested_actions:
        return False, authorized_actions, "authorization-reconciliation"
    if approval_actions < 1:
        return False, approval_actions, "approval-boundary"
    if privilege_leaks > 0:
        return False, privilege_leaks, "privilege-leak"
    assert authorized_actions + denied_actions + approval_actions == requested_actions and privilege_leaks == 0
    return True, authorized_actions, "least-agency-policy"
